name: live_replay_parity_sequence
description: Paired live-vs-replay fixture: a 7-tick sequence run through both the live math_engine.compute_breakeven_update (with previously_persisted_stop_level threaded) and through the autotuner replay path (which must also thread previously_persisted_stop_level after the fix). Both paths must produce bit-identical stop_trigger_level trajectories.
provenance: schema-derived + hand-derived arithmetic. All expected values computed from math_engine constants (HWM_HOLD_TICKS_THRESHOLD=5, BREAKEVEN_ACTIVATION_MIN=0.4, BREAKEVEN_ACTIVATION_MAX=3.0, BREAKEVEN_ACTIVATION_DEADBAND=0.2). No producer output hardcoded — expected values assert monotonicity and equality between live/replay paths.

ticks.derivation: return=1.5>=0.8 => hold_ticks=1, locked=False. Not locked: stop=base=-0.5. prev=None => ratchet no-op. Both paths: stop=-0.5
hold_ticks=2, locked=False. stop=base=-0.3. ratchet: max(-0.5, -0.3)=-0.3 (ratchet fires, new > prev). stop=-0.3
hold_ticks=3, locked=False. stop=base=-0.1. ratchet: max(-0.3,-0.1)=-0.1. stop=-0.1
hold_ticks=4, locked=False. stop=base=0.5. ratchet: max(-0.1, 0.5)=0.5. stop=0.5
hold_ticks=5 => LOCK FIRES (5>=5). locked_branch: stop=max(1.0,0.0)=1.0. ratchet: max(0.5,1.0)=1.0. stop=1.0
locked=True. locked_branch: stop=max(2.0,0.0)=2.0. ratchet: max(1.0,2.0)=2.0. stop=2.0
locked=True (latched). locked_branch: stop=max(1.0,0.0)=1.0. WITHOUT ratchet: stop=1.0 (DROP from 2.0). WITH ratchet: max(2.0,1.0)=2.0 (holds). This is the divergence tick.

assertion_contract.tick_6_critical: Both live and replay return 2.0 at tick_6 (not 1.0). Divergence is the current bug.
assertion_contract.note: expected_stop values for ticks 0-5 assert exact equality (ratchet and naive agree). Tick 6 asserts ratcheted value only.
